Baccharis macrophylla, also known as the River Paperbush, is a shrub native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis macrophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Baccharis macrophylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
